summary: Synthesize custom kinds from the committed lock's KindFactRows in gate() so a custom-kind requirement resolves instead of a false admissibility failure (cascade field report)

per.section: The lock and drift â€” one vocabulary

edit.description: In `gate` (and the read-arm twin at :416) the custom-kind corpus is hardcoded `let custom_kinds: Vec<CustomKindEntry> = Vec::new();` (:631/:416) and the kind-validation loop iterates `builtin_kind::definitions()` only (:571), so `by_kind`'s keys are `{skill,rule}` and a `require` naming a custom kind fails `requirement.admissibility` ("names kind X, which temper does not model") at roster.rs:177-184. Build the custom-kind slice from `declarations.kinds` (drift already parses these â€” verified), EXCLUDING rows whose name matches a built-in (those are the governs-overrides `effective_governs` already consumes at :755-765). Convert each surviving KindFactRow to a `CustomKind` via the new kind.rs ctor, run it through the existing generic `resolve_kind_units`/`kind_features` (:802/:847) and `assemble_by_kind`/`all_features` (:636/:641), and add it to the :571 validation loop so its floor clauses (from `declarations.clauses`, grouped by kind label) judge its members. Result: roster admissibility sees the kind, coverage counts real satisfiers on disk, conformance runs. Cut the now-false comments on contact per rust.md's exit clause: :626-630 ("no SDK path exists in the engine yet" / "no custom kind contributes members or edges") and the `Custom kinds retire with the KIND.md file format` lines at :398/:421/:888/:918.

Add a `CustomKind` constructor reconstructing a kind from a `drift::KindFactRow`'s five-fact residue: nameâ†’label, `governs_root`+`governs_glob`â†’`Governs` locus, `format`/`unit_shape`â†’layout/`member_document` shape, `activation`â†’registration. Field-level extraction primitives are compile-time-only (`specs/architecture/15-kinds.md`, "there is no kind file format") and absent from the row, so the reconstructed kind's extraction stays generic â€” frontmatter/section reads keyed by each floor clause's `field` column, which the kind-blind engine already does â€” never a per-kind primitive list. Sits beside the existing `CustomKind::new`/`from_surface_dir` (:190/:549).

tests.asserts: a harness whose committed lock declares a custom kind AND a requirement naming it does NOT emit a false requirement.admissibility "does not model" finding; the requirement resolves against the lock-declared kind, and the kind's members on disk are walked and counted so coverage reports genuine fill status only

acceptance: `temper check` (and its session-start reporter) synthesizes custom kinds from the committed lock's KindFactRows, so a `require` naming a custom kind resolves against its declared kind â€” no false `requirement.admissibility` finding â€” and the kind's members are walked, counted, and floor-checked; the session-start gate no longer cries wolf on any custom-kind harness.
notes: Cascade field report 07-06 â€” false blockers made cascade disable its SessionStart hook (law 3). Blocker BUILTIN-FLOOR-LOCK-PROJECTION shipped (5f68c8c). Agent trace 07-06: drift.rs already parses declarations.kinds; engine.rs is kind-blind (no edit); gate doesn't route through check.rs's Workspace. The fix is main.rs::gate feeding rows through the existing generic resolve_kind_units/assemble_by_kind + a KindFactRowâ†’CustomKind ctor in kind.rs.

summary: Whole-tree comment sweep â€” prose spec recaps to pointer tags, delete era + compliance narration, retired-tree citations, and dangling pending-tag refs per rust.md's taxonomy; no behavior change

gate.reason: Whole-tree sweep â€” must run solo (a broad src/+tests/ comment edit races any concurrent entry). Promoted to a solo open entry once CHECK-LOCK-KIND-ROWS ships and the queue is otherwise empty; promotion re-greps to finalize the exact residue set (including tests/).

edit.description: The `kinds/<name>/KIND.md` curated-tree citations (`//!` :5-7, :26, :60, :89) and the "any more" era narration: drop the citations (the tree is slated for physical deletion, a human chore), convert prose spec recaps to terse pointer tags (file path only), delete era narration. Module `//!` header to ~10 lines.

Era narration the projection left in its own header: `//!` :10-11 ("There is no package noun any more") and :95 ("the noun retired with the hand-written mirror") â€” commit-body material, cut per rust.md's exit clause. Header to ~10 lines.
The `packages/` curated-tree reference (:79) â€” cite the clause `cite` field / spec path only, drop the retired-tree pointer.

Retired `[edge.<target>]`/`[edge.*]` document-clause references and the dangling `READ-EDGE-UNIFY` pending tag (`//!` :23, :65, and :391): the `[edge.*]` construct shipped/retired and the tag is not in the queue â€” delete both per rust.md's exit clause (dangling pending-tag reference + retired-noun comment).

acceptance: Zero quoted spec section titles in src/ or tests/ comments; zero era vocabulary from 90-spec-system's migration trail; no comment referencing a pending tag that isn't in the queue; no citation of the retired `kinds/`/`packages/` curated trees or `[edge.*]` document clauses; module `//!` headers ~10 lines; cargo gates green â€” no behavior change.
notes: Ceremony item (John 07-06). rust.md's exit clause cuts comments on contact for chain-touched files (builtin.rs by the projection; main.rs's KIND.md comments by CHECK-LOCK-KIND-ROWS); this sweep is the residue in files the chain leaves untouched. Deferred, not blockedBy: a whole-tree edit races every open entry â€” promoted to solo open once CHECK-LOCK-KIND-ROWS ships and the queue is otherwise empty; promotion re-greps the full residue set (incl. tests/ + any main.rs remainder).

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels â€” SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle â€” fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist â€” a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published â€” the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. USPTO name screen due before launch.
